methodsAttempted.query: site:essex.ac.uk University of Essex generative AI policy ChatGPT academic integrity assessment guidance
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official student Artificial intelligence guidance page plus repository/publication and handbook candidates.

methodsAttempted.query: site:essex.ac.uk University of Essex artificial intelligence AI tools privacy data protection guidance students staff
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official staff AI tools guidance, the student AI guidance page, Zoom AI Companion guidance, and blog/news candidates.

methodsAttempted.query: site:essex.ac.uk AI policy University of Essex OR Generative AI Policy
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found an Essex Student Journal AI policy PDF and the AI Policy Observatory research project pages; these were not used for university-wide claims.
Verified official University of Essex pages under www.essex.ac.uk for student guidance, staff IT/business-process guidance, and Zoom AI Companion guidance.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found University of Essex student guidance on generative AI and academic offences, staff guidance for AI in business operations, and Zoom AI Companion guidance. This run did not identify a single central binding university-wide AI policy page beyond those scoped official guidance pages; research outputs, blogs, and journal-specific AI policy materials were rejected for institutional claim extraction.

stopConditions: Stop after official student, staff business-process, and approved-tool guidance pages are fetched and citation-ready claim/evidence candidates are prepared.
Do not fetch login-only services, search result pages disallowed by robots.txt, or third-party summaries.

artifacts.errorReason: Firecrawl returned page metadata and navigation but omitted the main policy body; static HTTP fetch was used for the source snapshot.
Firecrawl scrape timed out before completing; static HTTP fetch returned the page body.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Essex encourages students to explore and use generative AI tools responsibly, with attention to data safety, copyright, and academic honesty.

citation.citationTitle: Artificial intelligence guidance | University of Essex
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Essex student guidance states that AI-generated work should not be presented as a student's own assignment work and that using AI or other automated technology to produce material submitted as one's own original work is an academic offence.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Essex staff guidance for business operations says staff using generative AI in their work must comply with the University's Information Security Policy, IT Acceptable Use Policy, and relevant legal requirements.

artifacts.title: University of Essex AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Official University of Essex sources provide scoped guidance for student generative AI use in academic work, staff use of AI tools in business operations, and Zoom AI Companion. The run did not use research papers, research-project pages, blogs, or journal-specific AI policy materials as institutional policy evidence.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Claims are staged OpenClaw candidates and are not canonical public facts until reviewed and promoted.
The source-status claim reflects this run's official-source discovery; it should be revisited if Essex publishes a central AI policy or additional research-governance AI guidance.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
